description: The Singularity Update: Diff previews, YAML plugin system, multi-model routing, agent pipelines, self-healing error recovery, conversation forking, multi-perspective code review, natural language git, and live build monitoring. Features no other coding CLI has.

userStories.title: Add diff preview engine for file changes
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a colored unified diff of what the agent is about to change before it gets applied, so I can review changes visually.
acceptanceCriteria: Create internal/diff/diff.go with package diff
Implement func UnifiedDiff(oldContent, newContent, filePath string) string that produces a unified diff output with --- a/path and +++ b/path headers

Implement func ColorDiff(diffText string) string that wraps removed lines (starting with -) in red ANSI, added lines (starting with +) in green ANSI, and @@ hunk headers in cyan ANSI, using the color constants from internal/ui
Implement func FormatDiffStats(diffText string) string that returns a one-line summary like '3 additions, 2 deletions' by counting + and - lines

The diff algorithm should use a simple line-by-line comparison: split both strings by newline, find common prefix and suffix lines, and output the differing section with context (3 lines before/after)
Add unit-level verification: the function must handle empty old content (new file), empty new content (file deletion), and identical content (no diff) without panicking
Typecheck passes (go build ./...)

userStories.title: Integrate diff preview into write_file and edit_file tools
userStories.description: As a user, I want the agent to show me a diff preview before applying file changes when accept_edits mode is on.
acceptanceCriteria: In internal/agent/agent.go executeTool function, when a.acceptEdits is true and the tool is 'write_file' or 'edit_file', compute a diff BEFORE applying the change
For write_file: read existing file content (or empty string if new file), compute diff against the new content using diff.UnifiedDiff, display with diff.ColorDiff
For edit_file: read the file, compute what the content would look like after the replacement, compute diff between original and modified using diff.UnifiedDiff, display with diff.ColorDiff
After showing the diff, print the diff stats line from diff.FormatDiffStats
The existing accept/reject prompt (readline Y/n) must appear AFTER the diff is displayed, not before
If the user rejects (n), return 'User rejected the change' as the tool result instead of applying
When accept_edits is false, skip the diff display entirely and apply changes as before (no behavior change)
Import the new diff package in agent.go
Typecheck passes (go build ./...)

userStories.title: Add YAML plugin system for custom tool definitions
userStories.description: As a user, I want to define custom tools in YAML files that the CLI loads automatically, so I can extend the tool set without recompiling.
acceptanceCriteria: Create internal/plugins/loader.go with package plugins

Define a PluginTool struct: Name string, Description string, Parameters []PluginParam (each has Name, Type, Description, Required bool), Command string (shell command template with {{.param_name}} placeholders)
Implement func LoadPlugins(dir string) ([]PluginTool, error) that reads all *.yaml and *.yml files from the directory, parses them into PluginTool structs using gopkg.in/yaml.v3
Implement func (p PluginTool) ToToolDefinition() ollama.Tool that converts a PluginTool to an ollama.Tool definition with type 'function'

Implement func (p PluginTool) Execute(args map[string]interface{}) (string, error) that renders the Command template with the provided args using strings.ReplaceAll for each {{.param_name}} pattern, then executes it via exec.Command('bash', '-c', rendered) with a 120-second timeout
Add gopkg.in/yaml.v3 to go.mod (run go get gopkg.in/yaml.v3)
The plugin directory path is ~/.g-rump-cli/plugins/ — create it if it doesn't exist during LoadPlugins
Typecheck passes (go build ./...)

userStories.title: Integrate plugin system into agent and add /plugins command
userStories.description: As a user, I want plugins to be loaded at startup and listed with a /plugins command.
acceptanceCriteria: In cmd/root.go runREPL, after creating the agent, call plugins.LoadPlugins(filepath.Join(configDir(), 'plugins')) and store the result
Pass loaded plugins to agent via a new agent method: SetPlugins([]plugins.PluginTool)

In agent.go, add a plugins field ([]plugins.PluginTool) to the Agent struct. In New(), also add plugin tool definitions to toolDefsRaw by appending their ToToolDefinition() results to the tools.Definitions() slice before serializing
In agent.go executeTool, before the 'unknown tool' error, check if the tool name matches any plugin name and call plugin.Execute(args) if so
Add plugin tool names to the toolSpinLabel map with label 'Running plugin...'

In cmd/root.go handleCommand, add a '/plugins' case that lists all loaded plugins: name, description, and parameter count. If no plugins loaded, print 'No plugins loaded. Add YAML files to ~/.g-rump-cli/plugins/'
Add /plugins to the readline autocompleter and /help output
Typecheck passes (go build ./...)

userStories.title: Add multi-model config with fast/default/powerful tiers
userStories.description: As a user, I want to configure different AI models for different task complexities so the CLI uses fast models for simple reads and powerful models for complex reasoning.
acceptanceCriteria: In cmd/root.go Config struct, add a Models field: Models map[string]string `json:"models,omitempty"` with keys 'fast', 'default', 'powerful'
In loadConfig, if cfg.Models is nil, initialize it as an empty map

In cmd/root.go handleCommand /model case, extend the command: '/model tiers' shows current fast/default/powerful assignments, '/model fast <name>' sets the fast tier model, '/model powerful <name>' sets the powerful tier model, '/model <name>' (no tier prefix) sets the default tier as before
Add a new agent method SetModelTiers(tiers map[string]string) that stores the tier map on the Agent struct as a new field 'modelTiers map[string]string'
In cmd/root.go after creating the agent, call ag.SetModelTiers(cfg.Models) if cfg.Models is non-empty
Persist model tier changes to config via saveConfig when /model fast or /model powerful is used
Typecheck passes (go build ./...)

userStories.title: Add intelligent model routing in agent loop
userStories.description: As a user, I want the agent to automatically use the fast model for read-only tool calls and the powerful model for complex multi-tool planning, optimizing cost and speed.
acceptanceCriteria: In agent.go, add a method routeModel(toolCalls []ollama.ToolCall, turnCount int) string that selects the appropriate model tier

Routing logic: if modelTiers is nil or empty, always return a.model (default). If the previous response had ONLY read-only tool calls (read_file, glob, grep, list_directory, project_map, fetch_webpage, search_web), use the 'fast' tier model for the next API call. If turnCount > 5 (deep multi-step reasoning), use the 'powerful' tier model. Otherwise use the 'default' tier (which falls back to a.model if not set)
In the Run() loop, before building the ChatRequest, call routeModel with the previous turn's tool calls and current turn count. Set req.Model to the returned model
Add a Debugf log line when the model switches: 'Model routed: %s (tier: %s)'
If a tier model is not configured (empty string in map), fall back to a.model
Typecheck passes (go build ./...)

userStories.title: Add agent pipeline framework with YAML definitions
userStories.description: As a user, I want to define multi-step AI agent workflows (like review or TDD) as YAML pipeline files that execute specialized agent steps in sequence.
acceptanceCriteria: Create internal/pipeline/pipeline.go with package pipeline

Define Pipeline struct: Name string, Description string, Steps []Step. Define Step struct: Name string, Role string, Prompt string (template with {{.input}} placeholder), DependsOn string (optional, name of previous step whose output feeds in)
Implement func LoadPipeline(path string) (*Pipeline, error) that reads a YAML file into a Pipeline struct using gopkg.in/yaml.v3
Implement func LoadAllPipelines(dir string) ([]*Pipeline, error) that loads all *.yaml files from a directory

Implement func (p *Pipeline) Execute(ctx context.Context, input string, runStep func(ctx context.Context, stepName string, prompt string) (string, error)) (map[string]string, error) that executes each step in order, passing the previous step's output into the next step's {{.input}} template. The runStep callback is provided by the caller (agent) to actually run the AI. Returns a map of step_name -> output
For steps with DependsOn, inject that specific step's output as {{.input}}. For the first step (or steps without DependsOn), use the original user input
Typecheck passes (go build ./...)

userStories.title: Ship built-in pipelines and /pipeline command
userStories.description: As a user, I want to run /pipeline review to get a multi-perspective code review, /pipeline tdd to do test-driven development, and /pipeline refactor for guided refactoring.
acceptanceCriteria: Create internal/pipeline/builtins.go that defines 3 built-in pipelines as Go string constants (YAML format) and a func BuiltinPipelines() []*Pipeline that parses and returns them

Review pipeline (3 steps): step 'analyze' (prompt: 'Analyze the following code changes for correctness issues, logic errors, and potential bugs: {{.input}}'), step 'security' (prompt: 'Review for security vulnerabilities including injection, auth issues, data exposure: {{.input}}'), step 'summary' (prompt: 'Combine these review findings into a structured report with severity ratings. Correctness findings: {{.analyze_output}}. Security findings: {{.security_output}}')

TDD pipeline (3 steps): step 'write_tests' (prompt: 'Write failing tests for: {{.input}}'), step 'implement' (prompt: 'Implement the minimum code to make these tests pass: {{.write_tests_output}}'), step 'verify' (prompt: 'Run the tests and verify they pass. If they fail, fix the implementation: {{.implement_output}}')

Refactor pipeline (2 steps): step 'analyze' (prompt: 'Analyze this code for refactoring opportunities (duplication, complexity, naming): {{.input}}'), step 'refactor' (prompt: 'Apply the suggested refactorings surgically: {{.analyze_output}}')

In cmd/root.go handleCommand, add '/pipeline' case: '/pipeline' lists available pipelines, '/pipeline <name>' runs the named pipeline by collecting the next user input as the pipeline input, then calling the pipeline Execute with a runStep that uses delegate_task (or creates a temporary agent sub-call)
Add /pipeline to readline autocompleter and /help output
Typecheck passes (go build ./...)

userStories.title: Add self-healing error recovery pipeline
userStories.description: As a user, I want the agent to automatically try to fix failed tool calls using diverse strategies before giving up, so it can recover from common errors autonomously.
acceptanceCriteria: In internal/agent/agent.go, add a method attemptSelfHeal(ctx context.Context, toolName string, args map[string]interface{}, originalError string) (string, bool) that tries to recover from a tool failure

Healing strategies (tried in order): Strategy 1 (re-read): if the tool is edit_file and error contains 'not found in file', re-read the file and return a prompt suggesting the agent read_file first. Strategy 2 (permission fix): if error contains 'permission denied', try running 'chmod +rw' on the file path arg and retry the original tool. Strategy 3 (timeout bump): if error contains 'timed out' or 'deadline exceeded', retry the tool with double the timeout arg
In agent.go executeTool, after a tool returns an error, call attemptSelfHeal. If it returns (result, true), use that result instead of the error. If (_, false), return the original error as before
Add a healAttempts counter field on Agent (int) that tracks total healing attempts this session. Reset it in New(). Cap at 10 total healing attempts per session to prevent infinite loops
Add Debugf logging for each healing attempt: 'Self-heal attempt %d: strategy=%s tool=%s'
Typecheck passes (go build ./...)

userStories.title: Add error pattern learning store
userStories.description: As a user, I want the CLI to remember which error fixes worked so it can suggest proven solutions faster next time.
acceptanceCriteria: Create internal/learning/patterns.go with package learning
Define ErrorPattern struct: ErrorSubstring string, ToolName string, FixDescription string, SuccessCount int, LastSeen time.Time
Implement func LoadPatterns(path string) ([]ErrorPattern, error) that reads from a JSON file (default: ~/.g-rump-cli/patterns.json)
Implement func SavePatterns(path string, patterns []ErrorPattern) error that writes the patterns as indented JSON
Implement func RecordSuccess(patterns *[]ErrorPattern, errorSubstring, toolName, fixDesc string) that adds or increments a matching pattern

Implement func FindMatch(patterns []ErrorPattern, errorMsg, toolName string) *ErrorPattern that returns the best matching pattern (highest SuccessCount) where errorMsg contains the pattern's ErrorSubstring and toolName matches
In agent.go attemptSelfHeal, before trying strategies, call learning.FindMatch. If a match is found with SuccessCount >= 2, log 'Applying learned fix: %s' and try that fix first
After a successful heal in executeTool, call learning.RecordSuccess and save patterns
Typecheck passes (go build ./...)

userStories.title: Add conversation forking with /fork, /branches, and /switch
userStories.description: As a user, I want to fork the conversation to try different approaches and switch between them, like git branches for conversations.
acceptanceCriteria: In agent.go, add a new field 'forks map[string][]ollama.Message' initialized in New(), and 'currentFork string' (empty string = main branch)

Add Agent methods: ForkConversation(name string) error — deep-copies current messages into forks[name] and switches currentFork to name. SwitchFork(name string) error — saves current messages to forks[currentFork] (or forks['main'] if currentFork is empty), then loads forks[name] into a.messages, resets token cache. ListForks() map[string]int — returns fork names with message counts. CurrentFork() string — returns currentFork or 'main'
In cmd/root.go handleCommand, add '/fork' case: '/fork <name>' calls ag.ForkConversation(name), '/fork' with no args is same as '/branches'
Add '/branches' case that calls ag.ListForks() and prints each fork name, message count, and marks the current one with an asterisk (*)
Add '/switch <name>' case that calls ag.SwitchFork(name)
Add /fork, /branches, /switch to readline autocompleter and /help output
Typecheck passes (go build ./...)

userStories.title: Add /review command for multi-perspective code review
userStories.description: As a user, I want to run /review to get an AI-powered code review of my staged git changes from multiple perspectives (correctness, security, performance).
acceptanceCriteria: In cmd/root.go handleCommand, add a '/review' case
When /review is invoked, run 'git diff --cached' via exec.Command. If no staged changes, try 'git diff' for unstaged changes. If still empty, print 'No changes to review.' and return

Construct 3 review prompts from the diff: prompt1 = 'Review this code diff for CORRECTNESS. Look for logic errors, off-by-one errors, nil pointer dereferences, missing error handling, and incorrect assumptions. Be specific about line numbers. Diff:
' + diff, prompt2 = 'Review this code diff for SECURITY vulnerabilities. Check for injection attacks, hardcoded secrets, insecure defaults, missing auth checks, and data exposure. Diff:
' + diff, prompt3 = 'Review this code diff for PERFORMANCE. Look for N+1 queries, unnecessary allocations, blocking operations, missing indexes, and inefficient algorithms. Diff:
' + diff

Send each prompt sequentially to the agent via ag.Run(ctx, prompt) — prefix each with a section header printed to stdout: '=== CORRECTNESS REVIEW ===' in cyan, '=== SECURITY REVIEW ===' in red, '=== PERFORMANCE REVIEW ===' in yellow
After all 3 reviews complete, print a '=== REVIEW COMPLETE ===' footer in green
Add /review to the readline autocompleter and /help output
Typecheck passes (go build ./...)

userStories.title: Add /git natural language git command
userStories.description: As a user, I want to type '/git commit the auth changes with a good message' or '/git what changed today' and have the AI interpret my intent and execute the right git commands.
acceptanceCriteria: In cmd/root.go handleCommand, add a '/git' case that takes the rest of the input after '/git ' as a natural language instruction

Construct a specialized prompt: 'You are a git expert. The user wants to perform a git operation described in natural language. Interpret their intent and execute the appropriate git commands using the bash tool. IMPORTANT: Before any destructive operation (reset, force push, rebase), explain what you are about to do and ask for confirmation. User request: ' + the natural language input

Add additional context to the prompt: run 'git status --porcelain' and 'git log --oneline -5' via exec.Command and append the output as 'Current git status:
' + status + '
Recent commits:
' + log
Send this prompt to the agent via ag.Run(ctx, fullPrompt)

If no text follows '/git', print usage: 'Usage: /git <natural language command>
Examples:
  /git commit the auth changes
  /git what changed since yesterday
  /git create a branch for the new feature
  /git undo the last commit'
Add /git to the readline autocompleter and /help output
Typecheck passes (go build ./...)

userStories.title: Add /monitor for live build and test watching
userStories.description: As a user, I want to start a background file watcher that automatically runs builds/tests when files change and shows pass/fail status in my prompt.
acceptanceCriteria: Create internal/monitor/monitor.go with package monitor
Define Monitor struct with fields: running bool, mu sync.Mutex, stopCh chan struct{}, status string (one of 'idle', 'pass', 'fail', 'building'), lastError string, onChange func(status string)
Implement func New(onChange func(string)) *Monitor

Implement func (m *Monitor) Start(dir string, projectType string) error that launches a goroutine polling file mtimes every 2 seconds (using filepath.WalkDir, tracking a map[string]time.Time of file modtimes, skipping hidden dirs and node_modules). When any tracked file changes, set status to 'building', run the project's build/test command (Go: 'go build ./...', Node: 'npm test', Rust: 'cargo check', Python: 'python -m pytest --co -q'), set status to 'pass' or 'fail' based on exit code, call onChange with the new status
Implement func (m *Monitor) Stop()
Implement func (m *Monitor) Status() string that returns the current status string

In cmd/root.go, add a '/monitor' case: '/monitor' or '/monitor start' creates and starts a monitor, storing it as a variable in the REPL scope. '/monitor stop' stops it. '/monitor status' shows current status

When the monitor is running, modify the getPrompt() function to prepend a colored status indicator: green dot for pass, red dot for fail, yellow dot for building, gray dot for idle — using ANSI: e.g., green='\033[32m*\033[0m '
Add /monitor to readline autocompleter and /help output
Typecheck passes (go build ./...)
